# Nopal

> Cross-platform UI framework for OCaml using MVU (Model-View-Update) architecture. Build local-first applications targeting desktop, mobile, and web from a single OCaml codebase.

Nopal compiles to JavaScript via js_of_ocaml for web and Tauri webview targets. Desktop and mobile are served via Tauri. The framework is not a React-style VDOM framework — it uses a pure description type (`Element.t`) that renderers interpret directly.

Homepage: nopal.run

## Architecture

Every concern is a separate opam package with strict one-way dependency flow:

```
nopal_mvu       ← MVU core (Cmd, Sub, App) — no UI or platform deps
nopal_element   ← Element.t DSL — no platform deps
nopal_style     ← Typed layout/paint — no platform deps
nopal_router    ← Type-safe bidirectional routing — no platform deps
nopal_draw      ← 2D drawing DSL (Color, Paint, Path, Scene, Scale)
nopal_charts    ← Composable interactive charts (Bar, Line, Area, Pie, Scatter, Sparkline)
nopal_runtime   ← Lwd reactive integration + MVU loop
nopal_test      ← Pure OCaml test renderer — no js_of_ocaml
nopal_web       ← js_of_ocaml + Brr DOM renderer (only browser-dependent package)
```

Critical boundary: `nopal_element`, `nopal_style`, `nopal_test`, `nopal_router` must compile on native OCaml without js_of_ocaml. Application view code never imports `nopal_web` or `Brr`.

## Quick Start

### Setup

```bash
opam install . --deps-only --with-test
just build    # opam exec -- dune build
just test     # opam exec -- dune runtest
just          # build + test + fmt + lint (run before every commit)
```

### Minimal Counter App

**counter.ml** — Pure model, update, and view:

```ocaml
open Nopal_element
open Nopal_style

type model = { count : int }
type msg = Increment | Decrement | Reset

let init () = ({ count = 0 }, Nopal_mvu.Cmd.none)

let update model = function
  | Increment -> ({ count = model.count + 1 }, Nopal_mvu.Cmd.none)
  | Decrement -> ({ count = max 0 (model.count - 1) }, Nopal_mvu.Cmd.none)
  | Reset -> ({ count = 0 }, Nopal_mvu.Cmd.none)

let button_style =
  Style.default
  |> Style.with_layout (fun l ->
       { l with width = Fixed 64.0 } |> Style.padding 8.0 16.0 8.0 16.0)

let view model =
  column
    ~style:
      (Style.default
      |> Style.with_layout (fun l ->
           { l with cross_align = Center; gap = 8.0 } |> Style.padding_all 16.0))
    [
      text (string_of_int model.count);
      button ~style:button_style ~on_click:Increment (text "+");
      button ~style:button_style ~on_click:Decrement (text "-");
      button ~style:button_style ~on_click:Reset (text "Reset");
    ]

let subscriptions _model = Nopal_mvu.Sub.none
```

**main.ml** — Web mounting (the only file that touches browser APIs):

```ocaml
let () =
  let open Brr in
  let target =
    match Document.find_el_by_id G.document (Jstr.v "app") with
    | Some el -> el
    | None ->
        let body = Document.body G.document in
        let div = El.div [] in
        El.append_children body [ div ];
        div
  in
  Nopal_web.mount
    (module Counter : Nopal_mvu.App.S
      with type model = Counter.model
       and type msg = Counter.msg)
    target
```

## Core API Reference

### App Module (`nopal_mvu`)

Every Nopal application implements this signature:

```ocaml
module type App.S = sig
  type model
  type msg
  val init          : unit -> model * msg Cmd.t
  val update        : model -> msg -> model * msg Cmd.t
  val view          : model -> msg Element.t
  val subscriptions : model -> msg Sub.t
end
```

### Commands (`Cmd.t`)

Pure descriptions of side effects — never execute directly. The runtime interprets them.

```ocaml
Cmd.none                                      (* no effect *)
Cmd.batch : 'msg Cmd.t list -> 'msg Cmd.t     (* combine multiple commands *)
Cmd.perform : (('msg -> unit) -> unit) -> 'msg Cmd.t  (* synchronous effect *)
Cmd.task : (('msg -> unit) -> unit) -> 'msg Cmd.t     (* async deferred effect *)
Cmd.after : int -> 'msg -> 'msg Cmd.t         (* delay message by N ms *)
Cmd.map : ('a -> 'b) -> 'a Cmd.t -> 'b Cmd.t  (* transform message type *)
```

### Subscriptions (`Sub.t`)

Ongoing event sources managed by the runtime. All subscriptions are keyed for identity-based diffing.

```ocaml
Sub.none                                                        (* no subs *)
Sub.batch : 'msg Sub.t list -> 'msg Sub.t                       (* combine *)
Sub.every : string -> int -> (unit -> 'msg) -> 'msg Sub.t       (* timer, key + ms *)
Sub.on_keydown : string -> (string -> 'msg) -> 'msg Sub.t       (* global keydown *)
Sub.on_keyup : string -> (string -> 'msg) -> 'msg Sub.t         (* global keyup *)
Sub.on_resize : string -> (int -> int -> 'msg) -> 'msg Sub.t    (* window resize *)
Sub.on_visibility_change : string -> (bool -> 'msg) -> 'msg Sub.t
Sub.custom : string -> (('msg -> unit) -> unit -> unit) -> 'msg Sub.t  (* setup returns cleanup *)
Sub.map : ('a -> 'b) -> 'a Sub.t -> 'b Sub.t
```

### Element DSL (`nopal_element`)

`Element.t` is an exposed (not abstract) variant type so renderers can pattern-match exhaustively.

```ocaml
Element.empty                                                    (* renders nothing *)
Element.text : string -> 'msg Element.t                          (* plain text *)
Element.styled_text : text_style:Text.t -> string -> 'msg Element.t

(* Layout containers *)
Element.box    : ?style:Style.t -> ?interaction:Interaction.t -> ?attrs:(string * string) list -> 'msg Element.t list -> 'msg Element.t
Element.row    : ?style:Style.t -> ?interaction:Interaction.t -> ?attrs:(string * string) list -> 'msg Element.t list -> 'msg Element.t
Element.column : ?style:Style.t -> ?interaction:Interaction.t -> ?attrs:(string * string) list -> 'msg Element.t list -> 'msg Element.t

(* Interactive elements *)
Element.button : ?style:Style.t -> ?interaction:Interaction.t -> ?attrs:(string * string) list
  -> ?on_click:'msg -> ?on_dblclick:'msg -> 'msg Element.t -> 'msg Element.t
Element.input : ?style:Style.t -> ?interaction:Interaction.t -> ?attrs:(string * string) list
  -> ?placeholder:string -> ?on_change:(string -> 'msg) -> ?on_submit:'msg
  -> ?on_blur:'msg -> ?on_keydown:(string -> 'msg option) -> string -> 'msg Element.t

(* Media and scrolling *)
Element.image  : ?style:Style.t -> src:string -> alt:string -> unit -> 'msg Element.t
Element.scroll : ?style:Style.t -> 'msg Element.t -> 'msg Element.t

(* Identity for list reconciliation *)
Element.keyed : string -> 'msg Element.t -> 'msg Element.t

(* 2D canvas drawing *)
Element.draw : ?on_pointer_move:(pointer_event -> 'msg) -> ?on_click:(pointer_event -> 'msg)
  -> ?on_pointer_leave:'msg -> ?cursor:Cursor.t -> ?aria_label:string
  -> width:float -> height:float -> Scene.t list -> 'msg Element.t

(* Message transform for component embedding *)
Element.map : ('a -> 'b) -> 'a Element.t -> 'b Element.t
```

### Style System (`nopal_style`)

All styling is typed — no CSS strings in view code.

```ocaml
(* Layout *)
type direction = Row_dir | Column_dir
type align = Start | Center | End_ | Stretch | Space_between
type size = Fill | Hug | Fixed of float | Fraction of float

(* Colors *)
type color = Rgba of { r : int; g : int; b : int; a : float }
           | Hex of string | Named of string | Transparent

(* Borders *)
type border_style = Solid | Dashed | Dotted | No_border
type border = { width : float; style : border_style; color : color; radius : float }

(* Building styles *)
Style.default                                            (* sensible defaults *)
Style.with_layout : (layout -> layout) -> Style.t -> Style.t
Style.with_paint  : (paint -> paint)   -> Style.t -> Style.t
Style.with_text   : (Text.t -> Text.t) -> Style.t -> Style.t
Style.padding     : float -> float -> float -> float -> layout -> layout  (* T R B L *)
Style.padding_all : float -> layout -> layout
Style.hex   : string -> color
Style.named : string -> color
Style.rgba  : int -> int -> int -> float -> color
```

### Interaction (`nopal_style`)

State-based style overrides for hover, press, and focus:

```ocaml
type Interaction.t = {
  hover   : Style.t option;
  pressed : Style.t option;
  focused : Style.t option;
}
```

### Typography (`nopal_style`)

```ocaml
type Font.family = System_ui | Sans_serif | Serif | Monospace | Custom of string
type Font.weight = Thin | Extra_light | Light | Normal | Medium | Semi_bold | Bold | Extra_bold | Black

type Text.t = {
  font_family    : Font.family option;
  font_size      : float option;
  font_weight    : Font.weight option;
  line_height    : line_height option;     (* Lh_normal | Lh_multiplier f | Lh_px f *)
  letter_spacing : letter_spacing option;  (* Ls_normal | Ls_em f *)
  text_align     : text_align option;      (* Align_left | Align_center | Align_right | Align_justify *)
  text_decoration: text_decoration option; (* Underline | Line_through | Overline | No_decoration *)
  text_transform : text_transform option;  (* Uppercase | Lowercase | Capitalize | No_transform *)
  text_overflow  : text_overflow option;   (* Clip | Ellipsis | Wrap | No_wrap *)
  italic         : bool option;
}
```

### Routing (`nopal_router`)

Type-safe bidirectional routing:

```ocaml
Router.create : platform:(module Platform.S) -> parse:(string -> 'route option)
  -> to_path:('route -> string) -> not_found:'route -> 'route Router.t

Router.current  : 'route Router.t -> 'route                    (* read current *)
Router.push     : 'route Router.t -> 'route -> 'msg Cmd.t      (* push history *)
Router.replace  : 'route Router.t -> 'route -> 'msg Cmd.t      (* replace current *)
Router.back     : 'route Router.t -> 'msg Cmd.t                (* go back *)
Router.on_navigate : 'route Router.t -> ('route -> 'msg) -> 'msg Sub.t  (* subscribe *)
```

### 2D Drawing (`nopal_draw`)

Vector graphics DSL for `Element.draw`:

```ocaml
(* Colors — float 0.0-1.0 range *)
Color.rgba : r:float -> g:float -> b:float -> a:float -> Color.t
Color.rgb  : r:float -> g:float -> b:float -> Color.t
Color.hsla : h:float -> s:float -> l:float -> a:float -> Color.t
Color.of_hex : string -> (Color.t, string) result
Color.lerp : Color.t -> Color.t -> float -> Color.t
Color.categorical : Color.t array     (* 10 distinct colors *)

(* Paint fills *)
type Paint.t = Solid of Color.t
             | Linear_gradient of { x0; y0; x1; y1; stops }
             | Radial_gradient of { cx; cy; r; stops }
             | No_paint

(* Scene primitives *)
Scene.rect   : ?rx -> ?ry -> ?fill -> ?stroke -> x -> y -> w -> h -> unit -> Scene.t
Scene.circle : ?fill -> ?stroke -> cx -> cy -> r -> unit -> Scene.t
Scene.text   : ?font_size -> ?font_family -> ?font_weight -> ?fill -> ?anchor -> ?baseline -> x -> y -> string -> Scene.t
Scene.group  : ?opacity -> ?blend -> ?transforms -> Scene.t list -> Scene.t

(* Path building *)
Path.smooth_curve  : (float * float) list -> Path.segment list   (* Catmull-Rom spline *)
Path.straight_line : (float * float) list -> Path.segment list
Path.closed_area   : (float * float) list -> Path.segment list

(* Data-to-pixel mapping *)
Scale.create : domain:(float * float) -> range:(float * float) -> Scale.t
Scale.apply  : Scale.t -> float -> float
Scale.invert : Scale.t -> float -> float
```

### Charts (`nopal_charts`)

Composable interactive charts built on `nopal_draw`:

```ocaml
(* Bar chart *)
Bar.view : data:'a list -> label:('a -> string) -> value:('a -> float)
  -> color:('a -> Color.t) -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?padding:Padding.t -> ?x_axis:Axis.config -> ?y_axis:Axis.config
  -> ?format_tooltip:('a -> 'msg Element.t) -> ?on_hover:(Hover.t -> 'msg)
  -> ?on_leave:'msg -> ?hover:Hover.t option -> unit -> 'msg Element.t

(* Line chart *)
Line.view : series:'a Line.series list -> x:('a -> float) -> width:float -> height:float -> ...

(* Area chart — Stacked | Overlapping modes *)
Area.view : series:'a Area.series list -> x:('a -> float) -> ?mode:Area.mode -> ...

(* Pie/donut chart — inner_radius 0.0 = pie, >0.0 = donut *)
Pie.view : data:'a list -> value:('a -> float) -> label:('a -> string)
  -> color:('a -> Color.t) -> ?inner_radius:float -> ...

(* Scatter/bubble chart *)
Scatter.view : data:'a list -> x:('a -> float) -> y:('a -> float)
  -> ?radius:('a -> float) -> color:('a -> Color.t) -> ...

(* Sparkline — minimal inline chart *)
Sparkline.view : data:float list -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?color:Color.t -> ?stroke_width:float -> unit -> 'msg Element.t
```

Chart hover state: `Hover.t = { index: int; series: int; cursor_x: float; cursor_y: float }`

### Test Renderer (`nopal_test`)

Pure OCaml structural testing without a browser:

```ocaml
(* Render and inspect *)
Test_renderer.render   : 'msg Element.t -> 'msg rendered
Test_renderer.tree     : 'msg rendered -> node
Test_renderer.messages : 'msg rendered -> 'msg list

(* Query *)
Test_renderer.find     : selector -> node -> node option
Test_renderer.find_all : selector -> node -> node list
Test_renderer.text_content : node -> string

type selector = By_tag of string | By_text of string | By_attr of string * string
              | First_child | Nth_child of int

(* Simulate events *)
Test_renderer.click        : selector -> 'msg rendered -> (unit, error) result
Test_renderer.input        : selector -> string -> 'msg rendered -> (unit, error) result
Test_renderer.submit       : selector -> 'msg rendered -> (unit, error) result
Test_renderer.dblclick     : selector -> 'msg rendered -> (unit, error) result
Test_renderer.pointer_move : selector -> x:float -> y:float -> 'msg rendered -> (unit, error) result

(* Full MVU loop testing *)
Test_renderer.run_app :
  init:(unit -> 'model * 'msg Cmd.t) ->
  update:('model -> 'msg -> 'model * 'msg Cmd.t) ->
  view:('model -> 'msg Element.t) ->
  'msg list -> 'model * 'msg rendered
```

### Web Renderer (`nopal_web`)

Mount an app to the DOM (the only package with browser dependencies):

```ocaml
Nopal_web.mount :
  (module Nopal_mvu.App.S with type model = 'model and type msg = 'msg)
  -> Brr.El.t -> unit

(* Browser platform for routing *)
module Platform_web : Nopal_router.Platform.S
```

## Coding Rules

- Never `raise` for recoverable errors — return `('a, error) result`
- Never use catch-all `_` in match where the compiler can enforce exhaustiveness
- No `if/else` chains where `match` on a variant is correct
- No `mutable` fields without a justifying comment
- No inline helpers — helper functions live in dedicated modules with `.mli` files and tests
- No CSS strings in view code — all styling uses `nopal_style` types
- No business logic in `nopal_runtime` or `nopal_web` — they interpret and render only
- `App.update` never produces side effects directly — return `Cmd.t` instead
- Write tests first (TDD) — no `.ml` implementation without a failing test first
- Three test layers required: unit (Alcotest), structural (nopal_test), E2E (Playwright)
- Every PR touching runtime or web must benchmark against `bench/baseline.json` (no >20% regression)

## Project Structure

```
lib/
  nopal_mvu/         — Cmd, Sub, App — zero UI deps
  nopal_element/     — Element.t DSL — zero platform deps
  nopal_style/       — Style.t, Text.t, Interaction.t, Cursor.t
  nopal_router/      — Router.t, Platform.S
  nopal_draw/        — Color, Paint, Path, Scene, Transform, Scale
  nopal_charts/      — Bar, Line, Area, Pie, Scatter, Sparkline
  nopal_runtime/     — Lwd integration + MVU loop
  nopal_test/        — Pure OCaml test renderer
backends/
  nopal_web/         — js_of_ocaml + Brr DOM renderer
examples/
  counter/           — Hello world
  todomvc/           — Complex app with routing & localStorage
  kitchen_sink/      — Living visual reference for all features
  dashboard/         — Charts showcase
test/
  unit/              — Alcotest tests per package
  e2e/               — Playwright + TypeScript
bench/               — js-framework-benchmark + baseline.json
```

## Build Commands

```bash
just                   # build + test + fmt + lint (run before every commit)
just build             # opam exec -- dune build
just build-native      # verify DSL packages compile without js_of_ocaml
just test              # opam exec -- dune runtest
just serve-counter     # build + serve counter example
just serve-todomvc     # build + serve todomvc example
just serve-kitchen     # build + serve kitchen sink example
just serve-dashboard   # build + serve dashboard example
just bench             # run benchmark suite
just bench-compare     # compare against bench/baseline.json
```
